env_paras.num_jobs: Number of jobs, taking effect during training
env_paras.num_mas: Number of machines, taking effect during training
env_paras.batch_size: Number of parallel instances during training
env_paras.ope_feat_dim: Dimension of the raw feature vectors of operation nodes (0:status, 1:#machines, 2:proc_time, 3:#unscheduled_ops, 4:job_completion_time, 5:start_time, 6:slack, 7:critical_path_flag)
env_paras.ma_feat_dim: Dimension of the raw feature vectors of machine nodes (0:#neighboring_ops, 1:available_time, 2:utilization, 3:machine_load, 4:critical_path_load)
env_paras.show_mode: Result display mode (deprecated in the final experiment)
env_paras.valid_batch_size: Number of instances in single validation set

model_paras.in_size_ma: Dimension of the raw feature vectors of machine nodes (= ma_feat_dim; features: #neighboring_ops, available_time, utilization, machine_load, critical_path_load)
model_paras.out_size_ma: Dimension of the embedding of machine nodes
model_paras.in_size_ope: Dimension of the raw feature vectors of operation nodes (= ope_feat_dim; features: status, #machines, proc_time, #unscheduled_ops, job_completion_time, start_time, slack, critical_path_flag)
model_paras.out_size_ope: Dimension of the embedding of operation nodes
model_paras.num_heads: len() means of the number of HGNN iterations, and the element means the number of heads of each HGNN (=1 in final experiment)

pooling.method: Pooling method: 'topk' (generic learned), 'sagc' (scheduling-aware learned, uses ope features for scoring), or 'none'
pooling.ratio: k_mode='jobs': multiplier per job (e.g. 2.0 = keep 2*num_jobs nodes). k_mode='ops': fraction of operations to keep (e.g. 0.5 = keep 50%)
pooling.k_mode: 'jobs' = k relative to number of jobs (primary), 'ops' = k relative to total operations (secondary)
pooling.num_layers: Number of pooling/unpooling layers in the U-Net

train_paras.max_iterations: Maximum number of iterations for training
train_paras.save_timestep: Number of iterations to validate and save the network
train_paras.update_timestep: Number of iterations to update the network
train_paras.viz: Turn on visdom to visualize the training process
train_paras.parallel_iter: Number of iterations to replace training instances

test_paras.num_ins: Number of instances in this test set
test_paras.num_sample: Number of copied instances to sample for DRL-S
test_paras.num_average: Number of loops in DRL-G to calculate the mean
test_paras.public_ins: Instances from well-known benchmarks or not
test_paras.data_path: Relative path of this test set
